mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations down over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction phase reflects restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term equity rallies as recession fears subside.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production rebounds are negative or flat in > 55% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-recession regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to consider tightening policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses 230, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows, or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in new housing starts signals renewed investment in residential construction and related sectors, which stimulates economic activity and generates positive short-term equity market momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following housing starts increases are negative in > 50% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95 while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it indicates a supply-side contraction exacerbated by tight financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha in late-cycle regimes.
